summary: M5-PERFORMANCE-GATE-001 is CLOSED only after PR #118 passed CI, merged into main, local main synchronized to origin/main, and active child threads were closed. This was an evidence-only performance and stability gate: no product/runtime/content/tooling/docs/release/commercial/server/telemetry/protocol/save-schema/Electron IPC scope changed. QA accepted PASS and the desktop import/export caveat remains recorded for future platform bridge work.

tests_run.result: Quality, Web, and Browser Smoke passed in 1m47s; Windows Desktop Smoke Artifact passed in 47s.
Merged PR #118 and fast-forwarded local main to bd47faee8e6780a96f92402f47361012be40f3e1.

tests_run.command: git rev-parse HEAD && gh pr view 118 --json mergeCommit
tests_run.result: Local HEAD and PR #118 mergeCommit resolve to bd47faee8e6780a96f92402f47361012be40f3e1.
Both active task child threads returned completed previous_status and were closed.

decisions: Mark M5-PERFORMANCE-GATE-001 CLOSED because accepted evidence and review state are integrated into origin/main via PR #118.
Keep M5 formal passage out of scope; this is task-level closure only.
Carry the future desktop file import/export timing caveat into later M5 gate/release evidence if a bridge is added.
risks: P3 follow-up: future desktop file import/export work needs platform bridge timing because current bridge exposes only runtime/fullscreen.
P3 task wording: required_tests still mentions pnpm e2e while the repository actual script is pnpm test:e2e; actual script passed in writer/QA evidence.
requested_action: Commit closure, open closure PR, wait for CI, merge into main, then start M5-WEB-WINDOWS-DISTRIBUTABLE-001.
